bugs.title: cjkcodecs: MultibyteIncrementalDecoder.getstate() races with reset() on the decoder pending state
_zstd: ZstdCompressor.last_mode written plain under the lock but read lock-free via its Py_T_INT member descriptor
_decimal: concurrent hash() of a shared Decimal races on the cached hash field (dec_hash)
itertools: concurrent repr() of a shared count() races count_repr's plain read of lz->cnt against count_next's atomic advance
sys: addaudithook lazily creates interp->audit_hooks with no lock, racing should_audit on every audit event
list: non-atomic item/size reads race list_resize's atomic publish on a shared list (Py_SIZE / bytes_join / marshal readers)
list: concurrent list.sort() of a shared list races (binarysort rewrites the detached array with no critical section)
dict: readers of dk_nentries use plain loads instead of the existing LOAD_KEYS_NENTRIES macro, racing the atomic bump (object.__ge
readline: set_auto_history() writes the module-global should_auto_add_history flag without synchronization
frameobject/sys: a running frame's f_trace is written by trace_trampoline without the frame's critical section, racing the @critic
sys.monitoring: concurrent use_tool_id() races on the interpreter-global tool-name registry (monitoring_use_tool_id_impl)
_elementtree.TreeBuilder: concurrent feed of one shared builder races its unsynchronized parse state (self->data / self->last / se
interpreter finalization: handle_thread_shutdown_exception reads interp->threads.head in an assert() before _PyEval_StopTheWorld,
socket: PySocketSockObject.sock_timeout is read and written with plain non-atomic accesses (gettimeout() vs setblocking()), the on
sys.monitoring: the eval loop reads a code object's active_monitors.tools[] with no lock while _Py_Instrument replaces the whole s
TaskObj_dealloc leaves an _asyncio.Task refcount-0 while still GC-tracked across a _PyEval_StopTheWorld, aborting a concurrent gc.
descriptor: descr_get_qualname lazily caches descr->d_qualname with no lock -> write/write data race + leak on a shared method/get
types.GenericAlias: a shared ga_iternext (iter(list[int])) races gi->obj and Py_SETREF(gi->obj, NULL) -> data race + double-DECREF
dict: a shared dict iterator double-DECREFs di_dict in the exhaustion path -> negative refcount / use-after-free (dictiter_iternex

notes: fusil --tsan (ThreadSanitizer data-race) campaign, sibling catalog github.com/devdanzin/cpython-tsan-findings (53 races cataloged). 15 findings filed collectively in the umbrella python/cpython#153852 (9 new + 6 residual); 3 of them were picked up by community contributors as focused issues (TSAN-0006->#153908 johng/fixed, TSAN-0018->#153881 brijkapadia, TSAN-0035->#153935 deadlovelll). 4 standalone crash issues filed by devdanzin (TSAN-0033->#153809 asyncio Task, TSAN-0043->#154044 descr qualname, TSAN-0045->#154043 GenericAlias iter, TSAN-0053->#154130 dict iter). Corroboration comments on ~18 pre-existing issues (commented-not-authored) are EXCLUDED per catalog rules. Distinct from ft-review-toolkit (that is the C-extension FT static-review campaign).
